summary: Implemented realistic walk-the-book + slippage buffer for paper execution. Walk-the-book computes VWAP by consuming orderbook depth; slippage buffer (default 2 cents) is applied on top and fully configurable via env vars.

PAPER_SLIPPAGE_ENABLED.description: Enable walk-the-book + slippage model; when disabled, fills use best bid/ask without adjustment

PAPER_SLIPPAGE_CENTS.description: Slippage buffer in cents to apply on top of VWAP for both entry and exit fills

implementation_details.walk_the_book_algorithm: Consumes orderbook levels from best to worst, accumulating shares and USD spent to compute volume-weighted average price (VWAP). Tracks partial_fill flag when depth insufficient.
implementation_details.slippage_model: Additive cents buffer on top of VWAP (e.g., +$0.02 for entry, -$0.02 for exit). Conservative and parameterizable.
implementation_details.actual_fill_px: New field on Trade record (actual_fill_px) populated with the realized fill price including slippage, ready for attribution wiring.
implementation_details.logging: Per-fill log line includes: side | desired_size | vwap | fill_px | slippage_bps | partial_fill flag

example_fills.note: Fills entirely at level 1; VWAP matches best ask
Consumes levels 1-3 (0.54, 0.55, 0.56); VWAP averages across all
Consumes all available levels; partial_fill=true; worst VWAP due to deeper levels

example_orderbook.scenario: Synthetic Polymarket orderbook used for examples
example_orderbook.note: Real orderbooks fetched from Polymarket CLOB API; levels captured in OBSnapshot.ask_levels_yes/no and bid_levels_yes/no

integration_notes.orderbook_snapshot_extension: OBSnapshot now includes bid_levels_yes/no and ask_levels_yes/no (list of dicts with price/size). Populated in orderbook.py when fetching from Polymarket API.
integration_notes.trade_record_extension: Trade.actual_fill_px field added, populated in open_position() with the slippage-adjusted fill price. Ready for TRADE_ATTRIBUTION_WIRING_001 downstream.
integration_notes.env_var_pattern: Follows existing convention: PAPER_SLIPPAGE_ENABLED and PAPER_SLIPPAGE_CENTS read from environment with sensible defaults.
integration_notes.config_hash_updated: bot_core.py CONFIG_HASH includes both new config entries so config changes trigger rebuild signals.

next_steps: Verify walk-the-book behavior in live paper trading by checking fill logs for realistic VWAP and slippage.
Compare paper fills vs actual Polymarket fills to tune default slippage_cents (2.0 is conservative starting point).
Once TRADE_ATTRIBUTION_WIRING_001 lands, verify actual_fill_px field flows through to edge attribution (expected_edge_pct vs realized_pnl).
Monitor edge measurements: if still optimistic vs live, consider increasing PAPER_SLIPPAGE_CENTS in production config.
notes: Walk-the-book fills are most realistic when orderbook has sufficient depth. Polymarket markets vary; some thin books will hit partial_fill=true more often. Slippage model assumes conservative (not first-in-queue) position in limit order queue, suitable for paper validation. For live execution (future), would need to enhance with actual order queue position tracking.
